indicators.name: bed
indicators.rationale: The bed is a significant obstacle to setting up a computer workstation, as it takes up a large portion of the space and obstructs access to the workspace.

indicators.name: large bed with pillows
indicators.rationale: The presence of pillows on the bed makes it less suitable for setting up a computer workstation, as they can be used to prop up a laptop, but they also add clutter and reduce the available space.

indicators.name: rug
indicators.rationale: The rug on the floor can be a tripping hazard and may obstruct movement when setting up a computer workstation.

indicators.name: low lighting
indicators.rationale: The lighting in the room is low, which may make it difficult to see the computer screen clearly and comfortably.

indicators.name: no flat personal work surface
indicators.rationale: There is no flat personal work surface, such as a desk or table, which is necessary for setting up a computer workstation.

reasoning_summary: The indoor scene is marginally adequate for computer work (solo). The presence of a large bed and rug, along with low lighting and the absence of a flat personal work surface, make it challenging to set up a comfortable and efficient workstation. However, the room is not entirely unsuitable, as there is some potential for rearrangement to create a workspace.
